senses.definition: an arrangement based on which a user is given a private and personalized access to an online platform, application, or computer

examples.x: Setting up an account on the website allowed me to customize my preferences and access exclusive content.
You need to create an account to access the full range of features available on the app.
Her account was compromised, so she had to reset her password and update her security settings.
Logging into your email account enables you to send and receive messages securely.
With your account, you can track your orders, manage your subscriptions, and update your profile information.

senses.definition: a confidential string of characters that a user chooses to secure their account and prevent unauthorized access
(computing) an online service that enables users to add, edit and share bookmarks of web documents

examples.x: I use social bookmarking to keep track of all the articles I want to read later.
Social bookmarking allows me to find useful websites quickly when I need them.
She shared her favorite travel blogs through social bookmarking sites.
He organized his research papers using social bookmarking so he could access them anytime.
With social bookmarking, I can easily find new recipes that others have saved.

senses.definition: a set of related pages on the Internet located under a single domain name

a survey or questionnaire conducted through digital platforms or websites, allowing individuals to provide their opinions or vote on specific topics or questions using electronic devices connected to the internet

examples.x: I answered a quick online poll about my favorite movie genre.
The company sent out an online poll to gather feedback on their new product.
I participated in an online poll to vote for the best local restaurant.
They used an online poll to find out which event people were most interested in attending.

senses.definition: a web page on which an individual or group of people regularly write about a topic of interest or their opinions or experiences, usually in an informal style

examples.x: She started a travel blog to share her adventures around the world.
The tech blog provides daily updates on the latest gadgets and software.
He writes a food blog featuring recipes and restaurant reviews.
The company's blog offers insights into industry trends and best practices.
They collaborated on a blog to discuss environmental issues and solutions.

senses.definition: the act or activity of writing about different things and share them online on a web page

examples.x: Many businesses use blogging as a way to attract customers and promote their products.
After taking a course on blogging, he decided to start his own website to discuss fashion trends.
Blogging has allowed me to connect with people from all over the world.

senses.definition: an online publication that delivers news and information through a website or digital platform, providing articles, reports, and multimedia content to readers over the internet

examples.x: I prefer reading the web newspaper because it is always up-to-date with the latest news.
The web newspaper has a section dedicated to local news, which I find really helpful.
Many people now get their news from web newspapers instead of traditional print versions.
I subscribed to a web newspaper to access exclusive articles and reports.
The web newspaper often includes videos and images along with its articles, making it more engaging.

senses.definition: a type of blog or website that primarily focuses on curating and sharing links to interesting or valuable online resources, articles, websites, or multimedia content, providing a collection of links for users to explore and discover

examples.x: Instead of writing long posts, she updates her linklog with useful websites and links every week.
I discovered some great travel websites through a linklog I follow.
The linklog is updated daily, so there is always something new to check out.
He spends hours updating his linklog with the most interesting links he discovers during the day.
I came across a helpful tutorial in a linklog that showed me how to improve my website.

senses.definition: the virtual space where all the personal blogs are and the network of people communicating

examples.x: The blogosphere has become a powerful platform for people to express their opinions on various topics.
Many businesses are now using the blogosphere to reach potential customers and build brand awareness.
He discovered new recipes by exploring the blogosphere and reading food blogs.
The blogosphere was buzzing with excitement after the latest tech product launch.

senses.definition: the opening page of a website that introduces it and links the user to other pages

examples.x: She visited the home page to find the latest updates.
The home page features a menu for easy navigation.
He designed the home page to be user-friendly and attractive.
The home page includes links to the blog and contact information.

senses.definition: a computer program that enables the user to read or look at information on the Internet
a record or log of web pages and websites that a user has visited in a specific web browser

examples.x: You can check your browser history to find that article you read yesterday.
His browser history showed that he spent hours researching new gadgets.
I accidentally deleted my browser history, so now I cannot find the website.
The teacher asked us to clear our browser history before using the shared computer.

senses.definition: (computing) the first webpage that appears in response to a click on a link

examples.x: The landing page includes a sign-up form for users interested in receiving product updates via email.
Effective landing pages use clear headlines and compelling visuals to capture visitor attention.
A/B testing helps marketers optimize landing page performance by testing different layouts and content.
Landing page conversion rates indicate how well a campaign or promotion resonates with its target audience.
The call to action on the landing page encourages visitors to request a free trial of the software.

senses.definition: the practice of monitoring and recording user activities and behavior on websites or across the Internet

examples.x: Many online stores use web tracking to see which products are being viewed the most.
Web tracking can show how long visitors stay on a page and what parts of the site they explore.
Some people block web tracking to protect their privacy when browsing the Internet.
The company relies on web tracking to improve its website and make it easier to use.

senses.definition: (computing) data that a web server sends to a browser and receives if the user visits the website again, used for identifying or tracking the user's activities

examples.x: Websites use cookies to remember users' preferences and provide personalized experiences during their visits.
He cleared his browser's cookies to ensure his online activities weren't being tracked by advertisers.
The website prompted users to accept cookies as part of its privacy policy before accessing the content.
She disabled third-party cookies in her browser settings to enhance her online privacy.
The website's use of cookies allows it to analyze user behavior and improve its services over time.

senses.definition: the saved address of a website or file for easier and faster access
all the information in one part of a website

examples.x: He bookmarked his favorite web page to easily access it later.
I always check their web page for upcoming events and promotions.
I shared the link to the web page with my friends.
She learned how to code and design a web page on her own.
The web page displays the latest news headlines.

senses.definition: a computer program that searches the Internet and finds information based on a word or group of words given to it

examples.x: She used a search engine to find recipes for dinner.
The search engine displayed thousands of results in seconds.
Students often rely on a search engine to help with their research projects.
The company optimized its website to rank higher on the search engine results.
A good search engine can make finding information online much easier.

senses.definition: the activity of spending a lot of time online navigating through different websites
an online community that is focused on using a particular computer technology and sharing information about it

examples.x: She joined a user group online to get help with troubleshooting her computer software.
He became a member of the user group to connect with others who share his interest in digital photography.
I signed up for a user group to learn more about the latest updates in the app.
They started a user group to help new users understand the features of the website.

senses.definition: (computing) an occasion when a user goes to a particular webpage
a blog or a microblog that provides a real time commentary of an event as it is happening

examples.x: I followed the live blog to stay updated on the election results as they came in.
The sports website had a live blog covering the soccer match, updating every few minutes.
The live blog on the news website helped me keep track of the wildfire updates.
The team wrote a live blog to cover the press conference, keeping readers informed in real-time.
I checked the live blog during the game to see the latest scores and highlights.

senses.definition: a sequence of linked messages on social media, email, etc.

examples.x: Users can follow a thread of comments to understand different perspectives on a topic.
Participants in the thread debated the pros and cons of remote work.

senses.definition: any article or piece of information added to a blog, often including images, videos, etc.

examples.x: I read a really interesting blog post about healthy eating yesterday.
She wrote a new blog post about her trip to Japan.
I enjoy reading blog posts on technology to keep up with the latest trends.
He shared a blog post with tips on how to improve your productivity at work.
I plan to write a blog post about my favorite books next week.

senses.definition: a list of links on a blog to other websites that the owner thinks are useful

examples.x: I found some great new reading material by checking out the blogroll on my favorite blog.
He added my blog to his blogroll after we collaborated on a project.
She updated her blogroll to include some of the latest blogs in the fashion industry.
If you're looking for recommendations, check out the blogroll for a list of excellent resources.

senses.definition: (computing) a computer network or an online website where users can leave messages and read other people's posts

a graphical interface element typically found on websites or applications that allows users to enter keywords, phrases, or queries to search for specific information or content within the provided platform

examples.x: I used the search bar to find the recipe for chocolate cake.
She typed the name of the movie in the search bar to see when it was showing.
He clicked on the search bar to quickly find his favorite songs.
They added a search bar to the website, so it’s easier to find what you need.

senses.definition: a web-based platform or website where users can engage in discussions, exchange ideas, ask questions, and share information on specific topics of interest

examples.x: I joined an online forum to get advice about starting a small business.
She spends a lot of time on an online forum for book lovers.
The online forum helped me solve a tech problem I was struggling with.
He posted a question on the online forum and received helpful replies within minutes.
I found some great recipes on an online forum dedicated to cooking.

senses.definition: the act of voluntarily signing up or enrolling to receive regular updates, content, or services from a particular source or provider
a digital environment or software infrastructure that provides a foundation or framework for users or developers to create, interact, or exchange information, services, or content
information, criticism, or advice about a person's performance, a new product, etc. intended for improvement

examples.x: The teacher gave constructive feedback to help improve his writing skills.
Customer feedback is essential for improving product quality.
I appreciate your feedback on my proposal; it will help me refine it.
Feedback from the audience can help shape the performance.

senses.definition: a superior or exclusive level of quality, features, or benefits associated with a product, service, or experience
a feature on a website that enables the users to be notified of the updated information without logging on the website

examples.x: Podcast feeds allow listeners to automatically download new episodes to their devices.
Managing information overload requires curating feeds to focus on relevant and valuable content.

senses.definition: a website dedicated to the buying and selling of items that are put in an auction by users
the act of copying and saving digital files, such as documents, music, or videos, from the Internet onto a computer or other electronic devices

examples.x: The download took longer than expected due to the slow internet connection.
I completed the download of the new software update.
He received a notification confirming the download was successful.
You need to ensure you have enough space for the download.
The website allows you to initiate a download of the report.

senses.definition: the process of transferring or sending files, data, or content from a local device or computer to a remote server, network, or online platform
a continuously updated stream or list of news articles, updates, posts, or content that is delivered to users on a website or social media platform

examples.x: I checked my newsfeed to see what was happening with my friends today.
She spends hours scrolling through her social media newsfeed every day.
The newsfeed on my phone is filled with the latest headlines and updates.
I like to check my newsfeed for updates from my favorite blogs and websites.
His newsfeed was full of funny memes and pictures shared by his friends.

senses.definition: a unique identifier or name chosen by a user to represent themselves or their account in online platforms, websites, or social media

examples.x: His username is a combination of his initials and birth year.
Remember to write down your username somewhere safe.
She chose a unique username for her online account.
The website won't let me use that username; it's already taken.
Your username will be your email address.

senses.definition: a single screen or document on the internet that contains content such as text, images, videos, and links, often part of a website

examples.x: The contact page on the website includes an email address and phone number.
The page loaded slowly due to heavy traffic on the website.
The website has a page dedicated to customer reviews.
I found an interesting article on the news page of the site.
He shared the link to the blog page with his friends.

senses.definition: to deliberately and quickly move past or jump over certain sections or portions of media, such as audio tracks, video segments, or chapters

examples.x: During the movie, he decided to skip the intense scenes as they were too disturbing.
If you're short on time, you can skip the introductory chapters and jump straight to the main content of the book.
The streaming platform allows users to skip to specific scenes in a movie or TV show with ease.
If you find a section of the audiobook uninteresting, you can simply skip to the next chapter.
While reading the article, feel free to skip the footnotes if you're looking for a quicker overview.

senses.definition: a network of information that is accessible to people when they use the internet

examples.x: With the World Wide Web, you can learn almost anything from the comfort of your home.

senses.definition: a sensational or misleading online content designed to attract clicks

examples.x: Many websites use clickbait headlines to increase their traffic and ad revenue.
Clickbait articles often promise shocking or unbelievable stories to lure readers.
Users may feel disappointed or misled after clicking on clickbait links that do not deliver on their promises.
Social media platforms are known for hosting clickbait content that aims to go viral quickly.
Journalists and ethical bloggers avoid using clickbait tactics to maintain credibility and trust with their audience.
